Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me analyze the interviews: **Roles/Participants:** 1. Accounts Payable Clerk (Mary) 2. Purchasing Officer (Dan) 3. Department Manager (Carlos) 4. Accounts Payable Supervisor (Rita) 5. Supplier Relationship Manager (Karen) 6. Supplier (external)

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: **Main Process Flow (Happy Path):** 1. Receive invoice (usually by email) - AP Clerk 2. Check if associated with valid PO number - AP Clerk 3. If has PO: Log into accounting system - AP Clerk 4. Forward to Purchasing to confirm goods/services received - AP Clerk 5. Purchasing checks if invoice details line up with PO (quantities, prices, delivery charges) - Purchasing Officer 6. If correct: Send confirmation email back to AP - Purchasing Officer 7. Match invoice line items against PO in system - AP Clerk 8. If everything matches: Send to department manager for approval (system sends automatic email) - AP Clerk/System 9. Manager reviews and approves in system - Department Manager 10. Schedule for payment - AP Clerk

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: **Decision Points/Gateways:** 1. Has PO? (Start) - No PO: Send back to supplier requesting more details -> then presumably back to start when received? Or loop? - Has PO: Continue 2. Purchasing confirmation: Does invoice match PO? - Discrepancy: Email purchasing to clarify / hold invoice / contact supplier / ask for corrected version - Matches: Continue 3. AP Matching: Does invoice match PO? - Discrepancy: Email purchasing to clarify -> if cant resolve easily, escalate to supervisor - Matches: Continue to manager 4. Manager approval: - Approve: Continue to payment scheduling - Reject/Hold: Ask Mary to hold and investigate further -> presumably loop back after investigation 5. Exception handling (Supervisor): - Missing PO/informal services: Create retrospective PO or reject - Other discrepancies: Coordinate between departments, contact suppliers

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: **Exceptions/Variations:** - Missing PO: Send back to supplier (Mary), or Purchasing tells Mary to get PO info before proceeding (Dan). Karen helps get PO from supplier. - Discrepancy in Purchasing: Contact supplier directly or ask AP to hold until corrected version (Dan). - Discrepancy in AP matching: Email purchasing to clarify; if cant resolve easily, escalate to supervisor (Rita). - Manager sees suspicious stuff: Reject or ask to hold and investigate further. - No PO at all (services contracted informally): Supervisor works with department heads to create retrospective PO or reject. - Supplier Relationship Manager gets looped in for mismatches against agreed terms, missing POs, pricing discrepancies.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: **Artifacts/Inputs/Outputs:** - Invoice (received by email) - Purchase Order (PO) number / PO document - Accounting system entry - Confirmation email (Purchasing to AP) - System notification/email to Manager - Contract (Karen checks) - Revised/Corrected invoice - Retrospective PO

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: **Consolidating conflicting accounts:** - Mary says: If no PO, send back to supplier. If has PO, log, forward to purchasing. - Dan says: If PO missing, tell Mary she needs to get that info before proceeding. Also mentions contacting supplier directly for discrepancies. - Rita mentions: Missing PO or informal services -> create retrospective PO or reject. - Karen: Helps get PO or revised invoices. So the No PO path has variations: 1. Send back to supplier (Mary) 2. Escalate to supervisor for retrospective PO or rejection (Rita) 3. Karen helps obtain PO from supplier I should model this as an exception path that can loop back.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: **BPMN-like structure:** Pools/Lanes: - Accounts Payable (Mary) - Purchasing (Dan) - Department Manager (Carlos) - Accounts Payable Supervisor (Rita) - Supplier Relationship Manager (Karen) - Supplier (external) Actually, for textual BPMN, I can use a structured format with lanes. Let me draft the process: **Start Event:** Invoice Received (via email) **Lane: Accounts Payable Clerk** - Activity: Check for valid PO number ... [detailed draft continues]

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: **Lane: Purchasing Officer** - Activity: Confirm goods/services received ... **Lane: Department Manager** ... **Lane: Accounts Payable Supervisor (Rita)** ... **Lane: Supplier Relationship Manager (Karen)** ... **Lane: Supplier (External)** ... **Artifacts:** ... **Ambiguities to note:** ...

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me double-check if I missed anything: - Mary: ... [checks each interviewee] The structure is solid.
